text: The surgeon uses laparoscopic bulldog clamps to initially clamp the tumor-specific tertiary and quaternary segmental arteries. The gyrotus fascia is dissected completely, and the kidney is liberated from its attachments for mobilization during tumor excision and reconstruction. Bleeding interrupts the procedure, prompting a downgrade of the clamp from the quaternary to the tertiary branch. As the dissection progresses towards the back of the kidney, the perforating upper pole artery is clamped.

The surgeon progresses with the tumor excision by detaching the renal mass capsule from the normal kidney parenchyma, revealing the cleavage plane. Once the tumor is excised, the deep tumor bed is reconstructed using a III-O V-lock suture. The laparoscopic bulldog clamps are removed, and warm ischemia time is noted as thirty-three minutes. Additional III-O V-lock sutures are used to achieve hemostasis, and a Tacosil hemostatic patch is applied to manage residual bleeding from the large tumor bed.
